preamble: Low-front vowel iotation occurs when [æ] loses its syllabic status and becomes a front glide [j], often in contexts where it precedes another vowel.

examples.note: Reduction of [æ] to a glide in fast connected speech.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
